gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: delegateClaim missing ROLLER_ROLE guard at Portal dispatcher level allows unprivileged LP fee extraction
candidate.claimed_root_cause: Portal.delegateClaim applies only onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH) and no onlyRole(ROLLER_ROLE) or onlyRole(DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE); if PORTAL_ROLLER implementation also lacks a caller role check, any EOA can trigger LP fee collection to the beneficiary
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged EOA triggers LP fee collection; if attacker controls the beneficiary address, they receive LP fees without entitlement

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER implementation address
live_preconditions.observed_value: not present in live_context.json; PORTAL_ROLLER is an immutable set at construction time in PortalBase

live_preconditions.name: PORTAL_ROLLER source code or bytecode caller-role check
live_preconditions.observed_value: PORTAL_ROLLER source not present in repository; only out/IPortal.sol/IRoller.json (ABI artifact) exists; ROLLER_ROLE grep returns zero matches across all src/ files
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: cast etherscan-source <PORTAL_ROLLER_address> --chain bsc  OR  cast disassemble $(cast code <PORTAL_ROLLER_address> --rpc-url $BSC_RPC) | grep -A5 'CALLER'

live_preconditions.name: Token with launched DEX pool and accumulated LP fees whose beneficiary attacker controls
live_preconditions.observed_value: not enumerated in live_context.json; V4LPFeesCollected events and _tokenBeneficiaries mapping keys not captured

live_preconditions.name: setTokenBeneficiary access control (whether attacker can set beneficiary to self)
live_preconditions.observed_value: IPortal.sol NatSpec states 'Allows the default admin to change the beneficiary' — setTokenBeneficiary appears admin-only, so attacker must be original token creator/beneficiary

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Only guard is onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); no ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE check at the Portal dispatcher level; confirmed by source read
NatSpec explicitly states 'Only the roller or default admin can call this function', confirming the role restriction is intended design, not optional
GUARDIAN_ROLE, TAX_MANAGER_ROLE, TAX_GUARDIAN_ROLE, TOKEN_FLAP_FEE_SETTER_ROLE, MODERATOR_ROLE are defined; ROLLER_ROLE is absent — grep across all src/ confirms zero matches for ROLLER_ROLE
Only roller-related artifact in repo; no roller implementation source is present, making it impossible to confirm or deny whether the roller enforces a caller check internally

attacker_inputs: token: address of a launched token whose _tokenBeneficiaries entry the attacker controls (attacker is original creator/beneficiary)
call_sequence: 1. Attacker calls Portal.delegateClaim(token) from unprivileged EOA
3. _delegateToImpl(PORTAL_ROLLER) executes roller's delegateClaim logic in Portal storage context
4. IF roller has no caller role check: roller collects LP fees and sends to _tokenBeneficiaries[token] (attacker-controlled address)
state_before: token has launched to DEX with accumulated LP fees
CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH bit is set (protocol not halted)
state_after: LP fees transferred to attacker-controlled beneficiary address
attacker receives token and/or ETH/BNB LP fee proceeds

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: LP fees accumulated in DEX pool for a launched token (token amount + ETH/BNB quote amount)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: LP fee proceeds sent to attacker-controlled beneficiary without requiring ROLLER_ROLE
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: LP fees that should only be claimable by an authorized roller or admin are extracted by an unprivileged caller
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: NatSpec mandates ROLLER_ROLE or DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE; an unprivileged EOA has no entitlement to trigger this claim path; if roller lacks internal check, the Portal-level guard is the only barrier and it is absent

setup: Fork BSC at block 108382650 against proxy 0xe2ce6ab80874fa9fa2aae65d277dd6b8e65c9de0
Resolve PORTAL_ROLLER address from immutable storage or constructor args of implementation 0xeb6c62d1885c421797d0c494e693c90d4b54125a
Query V4LPFeesCollected events to identify a token with accumulated LP fees
Confirm _tokenBeneficiaries[token] — use a token the attacker created so beneficiary is attacker-controlled, or verify setTokenBeneficiary is also callable without admin role
Record ETH and token balances of beneficiary before call
expected_assertions: If call reverts with access-control error from roller: finding is INVALID — REJECT
If call succeeds and beneficiary ETH/token balance increases: finding is VALID — confirm delta equals LP fees collected
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: Call reverts with role-based revert from PORTAL_ROLLER implementation, confirming the roller enforces the caller check internally and the Portal-level omission is harmless
